name: Utah Consumer Privacy Act (UCPA)
summary: The Utah Consumer Privacy Act establishes consumer data privacy rights for Utah residents and applies to data controllers and processors conducting business in Utah. It is considered more business-friendly than other state privacy laws, with higher applicability thresholds requiring annual revenue of $25 million or more, and either controlling or processing data of 100,000 or more consumers or deriving over 50% of gross revenue from the sale of personal data of 25,000 or more consumers.
keyRequirements: Right to access and confirm whether a controller is processing personal data
Right to delete personal data provided by the consumer to the controller
Right to obtain a portable copy of personal data in a readily usable format
Right to opt out of the sale of personal data or targeted advertising
Consent required before processing sensitive data
Clear and conspicuous privacy notice with specified content requirements
Controllers must establish and respond to consumer requests within 45 days
penalties: Enforced exclusively by the Utah Attorney General. Civil penalties up to $7,500 per violation. 30-day cure period with no sunset provision. No private right of action.
